docs.title: Green Finance Professional Certificate
docs.text: Academic course generation system for 8-week Green Finance course with learning-goal-driven pedagogy, Beamer LaTeX slides, and interactive React web application.

docs.title: Live Interactive App
docs.text:
🌐 https://digital-ai-finance.github.io/Green-Finance

Interactive Week 1 learning platform with 30 slides, charts, and self-assessment.

docs.title: Course Structure
docs.text:
• Duration: 8 weeks
  • Format: Academic professional certificate
  • Pedagogy: Learning-goal-driven (3 goals per week)
  • Delivery: Beamer PDF slides + Interactive web app

docs.title: Week 1 Status
docs.text: ✅ Complete - 37 slides, 17 charts, fully validated - Core: 30 slides (3 learning goals × 10 slides) - Supplementary: 7 empirical validation slides - Interactive React app deployed

docs.title: Weeks 2-8 Status
docs.text: ⏳ Ready for generation using proven Week 1 template

docs.title: View Interactive App
docs.text: Visit: https://digital-ai-finance.github.io/Green-Finance

docs.title: Run Locally
docs.text:
cd react-app
npm install
npm start
# Opens at http://localhost:3000

docs.title: Compile LaTeX Slides
docs.text:
# Compile Week 1
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode 20251121_2306_Week1_v2_GreenFinanceFoundations.tex
pdflatex -interaction=nonstopmode 20251121_2306_Week1_v2_GreenFinanceFoundations.tex

# Clean up auxiliary files
Move-Item -Path *.aux,*.log,*.out,*.nav,*.toc,*.snm -Destination temp\ -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue

docs.title: Generate Charts
docs.text:
# Generate all Week 1 charts
Get-ChildItem charts\week1\*.py | ForEach-Object { python $_.FullName }

# Generate Graphviz diagrams (requires Graphviz installed)
Get-ChildItem charts\week1\*.dot | ForEach-Object {
    dot -Tpdf $_.FullName -o ($_.FullName -replace '\.dot$','.pdf')
}

docs.title: Repository Structure
docs.text:
Green-Finance/
├── react-app/              # Interactive web app (Week 1)
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── components/     # React components
│   │   ├── charts/         # Interactive chart components
│   │   └── data/           # Course content (slides)
│   ├── DEPLOYMENT.md       # Deployment guide
│   └── package.json
│
├── charts/week1/           # Chart generation scripts
│   ├── week1_v2_goal1_*.py     # Goal 1 charts (matplotlib)
│   ├── week1_v2_goal2_*.py     # Goal 2 charts (matplotlib)
│   ├── week1_v2_goal3_*.py     # Goal 3 charts (matplotlib)
│   └── *.dot                    # Graphviz diagrams
│
├── agents/                 # Multi-agent system specs
│   ├── AGENT_3_ContentPlanner_v2.md
│   ├── AGENT_4_SlideGenerator_v2_LaTeXTemplates.md
│   └── README_v2.md
│
├── *.tex                   # LaTeX source files
├── *.pdf                   # Compiled slide PDFs
├── template_beamer_final.tex   # Madrid theme template
├── CLAUDE.md               # Project documentation
├── COURSE_GENERATOR_v2.md  # Generation system spec
└── DEPLOY_QUICKSTART.md    # Deployment quick start

docs.title: Interactive Web App
docs.text:
• ✅ 30 slides with keyboard navigation
  • ✅ 3 learning goals with progress tracking
  • ✅ Interactive charts (Recharts)
  • ✅ Self-assessment quizzes
  • ✅ LocalStorage persistence
  • ✅ Mobile responsive
  • ✅ GitHub Pages deployment

docs.title: LaTeX Slides (v2.0)
docs.text:
• ✅ Madrid theme (8pt, 16:9)
  • ✅ 3 learning goals per week
  • ✅ Goal-driven narrative structure
  • ✅ 4 specialized slide types
  • ✅ 17 charts (33%+ ratio)
  • ✅ Bottom notes with goal tracking

docs.title: Multi-Agent System
docs.text:
• ✅ Course Orchestrator
  • ✅ Guidelines Expert
  • ✅ Content Planner v2.0
  • ✅ Slide Generator v2.0
  • ✅ YAML-based communication

docs.title: Web App
docs.text:
• React 18.2
  • Material-UI 5.14
  • Recharts 2.8 (charts)
  • Framer Motion 10.16 (animations)
  • D3 7.8 (advanced visualizations)

docs.title: Slides & Charts
docs.text:
• LaTeX Beamer (Madrid theme)
  • Python 3.x + matplotlib
  • Graphviz (diagrams)
  • pdflatex

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• GitHub Pages (automatic)
  • GitHub Actions (CI/CD)

docs.title: Goal 1: Market Microstructure Theory
docs.text:
Understand the theoretical foundations explaining why green finance markets exist and how they function.

Slides: 1-10 | Type: Theoretical | Charts: Ecosystem diagrams

docs.title: Goal 2: Quantify Market Size & Growth
docs.text:
Quantify the size, growth trajectory, and composition of global green finance markets using empirical data.

Slides: 11-20 | Type: Quantitative | Charts: Time series, distributions

docs.title: Goal 3: Derive Pricing Models
docs.text:
Derive and apply mathematical models for pricing green financial instruments, incorporating greenium and ESG factors.

Slides: 21-30 | Type: Mathematical | Charts: Yield curves, risk-return

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• CLAUDE.md - Complete project documentation
  • DEPLOY_QUICKSTART.md - Deploy in 3 steps
  • react-app/DEPLOYMENT.md - Full deployment guide
  • COURSE_GENERATOR_v2.md - System specification
  • agents/README_v2.md - Multi-agent architecture

docs.title: Automatic (GitHub Actions)
docs.text:
1. Enable GitHub Pages: Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions
  2. Push changes: git push origin main
  3. Visit: https://digital-ai-finance.github.io/Green-Finance

See DEPLOY_QUICKSTART.md for detailed steps.

docs.title: Manual
docs.text:
cd react-app
npm run deploy

docs.title: Add New Week
docs.text:
1. Create content outline: weekN_v2_content_outline.yaml
  2. Generate charts: python charts/weekN/*.py
  3. Generate LaTeX: Use AGENT_4_SlideGenerator_v2_LaTeXTemplates.md
  4. Compile: pdflatex YYYYMMDD_HHMM_WeekN_Title.tex (2x)
  5. Validate: 30 slides, 10+ charts, 3 goals

docs.title: Update React App
docs.text:
1. Edit content: react-app/src/data/week1Slides.js
  2. Test locally: npm start
  3. Commit and push: Auto-deploys via GitHub Actions

docs.title: Per Week Requirements
docs.text:
• Core slides: 30 (3 goals × 10 slides)
  • Charts: 10-11 minimum
  • Chart ratio: ≥33%
  • Learning goals: Exactly 3 (typed with narrative roles)
  • Statistics: All verified via web search

docs.title: Color Scheme (Consistent)
docs.text:
• Primary (mlpurple): #3333B2
  • Secondary (mllavender): #ADADE0
  • Success (mlgreen): #2CA02C
  • Warning (mlorange): #FF7F0E

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js 18+ (for React app)
  • Python 3.x (for charts)
  • pdflatex (TeX Live or MiKTeX)
  • Graphviz (for diagrams)
  • Git (version control)

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: This is an academic project. For questions or improvements: 1. Open an issue 2. Submit a pull request 3. Contact: Digital-AI-Finance organization

docs.title: NLP Course 2025: From N-grams to Transformers
docs.text: A comprehensive Natural Language Processing course covering statistical foundations through modern transformer architectures. Build ChatGPT from scratch!

docs.title: Quick Start (3 Steps)
docs.text:
# 1. Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/josterri/2025_NLP_Lectures.git
cd 2025_NLP_Lectures

# 2. Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# 3. Start learning!
jupyter lab NLP_slides/week02_neural_lm/lab/week02_word_embeddings_lab.ipynb

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
This course takes you from foundational statistical methods to state-of-the-art neural architectures:

  • Weeks 1-2: Statistical language models and word embeddings (Word2Vec, GloVe)
  • Weeks 3-4: Sequential models (RNN/LSTM) and sequence-to-sequence with attention
  • Weeks 5-7: Transformers, BERT, GPT, and advanced architectures
  • Weeks 8-10: Tokenization, decoding strategies, and fine-tuning
  • Weeks 11-12: Efficiency optimization and ethical AI deployment

By the end, you'll build a working transformer from scratch and understand the architecture behind ChatGPT and Claude.

docs.title: Core Materials (12 Weeks)
docs.text: Each week includes: - Presentation: LaTeX/Beamer slides with optimal readability - Lab Notebook: Interactive Jupyter notebook with hands-on exercises - Handouts: Pre-class discovery exercises and post-class technical practice

docs.title: Supplementary Modules
docs.text:
• Neural Network Primer: Zero pre-knowledge intro to neural networks
  • LSTM Primer: Comprehensive deep dive into LSTM architecture (32 slides)
  • Embeddings Module: Standalone word embedding module with 3D visualizations

docs.title: Total Content
docs.text:
• 60+ presentations (including versions and supplements)
  • 12 interactive lab notebooks
  • 40+ handout documents
  • 100+ Python-generated figures
  • 8 progressive visualization notebooks

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Required:
  • Python 3.8 or higher
  • Basic linear algebra (vectors, matrices)
  • Basic probability theory

  • Comfortable with Python programming

  • Helpful but not required:

  • PyTorch experience
  • Understanding of backpropagation
  • Machine learning fundamentals

New to neural networks? Start with our Neural Network Primer module before Week 2.

docs.title: Option 1: pip (Recommended)
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Option 2: conda
docs.text:
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate nlp2025

docs.title: GPU Support
docs.text:
For GPU acceleration (recommended for Weeks 5+): 

# CUDA 11.8
pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118

# CUDA 12.1
pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121

See INSTALLATION.md for detailed setup instructions and troubleshooting.

docs.title: Week-by-Week Guide
docs.text: Full navigation with topics, prerequisites, and learning objectives: COURSE_INDEX.md

docs.title: Week Highlights
docs.text: Week Topic Key Concepts Lab 1 Foundations N-grams, perplexity, statistical LM - 2 Word Embeddings Word2Vec, GloVe, neural LM Implement embeddings 3 RNN/LSTM Sequential models, BPTT Build LSTM from scratch 4 Seq2Seq Attention mechanism, translation Machine translation 5 Transformers Self-attention, multi-head Build transformer 6 Pre-trained BERT, GPT, transfer learning Fine-tune BERT 7 Advanced T5, GPT-3, scaling laws Experiment with GPT 8 Tokenization BPE, WordPiece, SentencePiece Implement tokenizer 9 Decoding Beam, sampling, nucleus, contrastive Compare 6 methods 10 Fine-tuning LoRA, prompt engineering Adapt models 11 Efficiency Quantization, distillation Optimize models 12 Ethics Bias, fairness, safety Measure bias

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
├── NLP_slides/
│   ├── week01_foundations/      # Week 1: Statistical LM
│   ├── week02_neural_lm/        # Week 2: Word embeddings
│   ├── week03_rnn/              # Week 3: RNN/LSTM/GRU
│   ├── ...                      # Weeks 4-12
│   ├── nn_primer/               # Neural network primer
│   ├── lstm_primer/             # LSTM deep dive
│   └── common/                  # Shared templates and utils
├── embeddings/                  # Standalone embeddings module
├── exercises/                   # Additional practice
├── figures/                     # Shared visualizations
├── requirements.txt             # Python dependencies
├── environment.yml              # Conda environment
└── COURSE_INDEX.md              # Full course navigation

docs.title: Key Learning Milestones
docs.text:
• ✅ After Week 2: Understand and implement word embeddings
  • ✅ After Week 3: Build RNN and LSTM from scratch
  • ✅ After Week 5: Comprehend transformer architecture completely
  • ✅ After Week 6: Fine-tune pre-trained models (BERT, GPT)
  • ✅ After Week 9: Control text generation quality and diversity
  • ✅ After Week 12: Deploy models responsibly with ethical considerations

docs.title: Run a Lab Notebook
docs.text:
# Start Jupyter Lab
jupyter lab

# Navigate to a week's lab folder
cd NLP_slides/week05_transformers/lab
jupyter notebook week05_transformer_lab.ipynb

docs.title: Compile a Presentation
docs.text:
cd NLP_slides/week02_neural_lm/presentations
pdflatex week02_neural_lm.tex

docs.title: Generate Figures
docs.text:
cd NLP_slides/week05_transformers/python
python generate_week05_optimal_charts.py

docs.title: Testing the Course
docs.text:
Test all lab notebooks for execution: 

python test_notebooks.py

This validates that all 12 lab notebooks execute correctly in your environment.

docs.title: Standard 12-Week Semester
docs.text:
• One week per topic
  • Weekly labs and assignments
  • Suitable for undergraduate/graduate courses

docs.title: Intensive 8-Week Course
docs.text:
• Combine Weeks 1-2, skip some advanced topics
  • Accelerated pace for bootcamps
  • Focus on core transformer concepts

docs.title: Self-Paced Learning
docs.text:
• Progress at your own speed
  • Complete prerequisite modules first
  • Focus on labs and hands-on practice

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• COURSE_INDEX.md - Complete week-by-week navigation
  • INSTALLATION.md - Detailed setup instructions
  • CLAUDE.md - Development guide and conventions
  • status.md - Project status and completion tracking
  • changelog.md - Change history

docs.title: Support and Resources
docs.text:
• Issues: Report problems at GitHub Issues
  • Prerequisites: Check the Neural Network Primer if you're new to deep learning
  • GPU Requirements: Most labs work on CPU; Weeks 5+ benefit from GPU

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Areas for contribution: - Additional exercises and examples - Translations to other languages - MSc-level challenge problems - Bug fixes and improvements

docs.title: License
docs.text: This course is released under the MIT License. See LICENSE for details.

docs.title: Acknowledgments
docs.text:
Course materials developed with pedagogical focus on: - Discovery-based learning - Concrete-to-abstract progression - Hands-on implementation - Real-world applications

Built with LaTeX/Beamer, Python, PyTorch, and Jupyter.

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use these materials in your course or research, please cite:

@misc{nlp2025course,
  title={NLP Course 2025: From N-grams to Transformers},
  author={Joerg Osterrieder},
  year={2025},
  url={https://github.com/josterri/2025_NLP_Lectures}
}

Ready to start? Check INSTALLATION.md for setup, then dive into Week 2's word embeddings lab!

Questions? See COURSE_INDEX.md for complete navigation and prerequisites.

docs.title: Backtesting Framework
docs.text: A high-performance, event-driven backtesting framework for trading strategies with realistic market simulation.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Event-driven architecture
  • Realistic order execution simulation
  • Transaction cost modeling
  • Slippage and market impact
  • Multiple asset classes (stocks, futures, options, crypto)
  • Portfolio-level backtesting
  • Risk analytics and reporting
  • Parallel processing support

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Backtest 10 years of daily data: < 2 seconds
  • Minute-level data: < 30 seconds
  • Supports multi-asset portfolios (1000+ securities)

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from backtesting import Backtest, Strategy

class MyStrategy(Strategy):
    def init(self):
        pass

    def next(self):
        if self.data.Close[-1] > self.data.Close[-2]:
            self.buy()

bt = Backtest(data, MyStrategy)
results = bt.run()
bt.plot()

docs.title: Explainable Credit Risk Prediction
docs.text: Repository for "Explainable Neural Networks for Credit Risk Assessment" (SSRN:3456789).

docs.title: Authors
docs.text:
• Maria Garcia (Financial Analytics Lab)
  • Robert Chen (AI Research Center)

docs.title: Abstract
docs.text: We develop an explainable neural network architecture for credit risk prediction that achieves state-of-the-art performance while providing interpretable feature importance scores using SHAP values.

docs.title: Published Paper
docs.text:
• SSRN: https://ssrn.com/abstract=3456789
  • DOI: 10.2139/ssrn.3456789

docs.title: Dataset
docs.text: We use the German Credit Dataset and a proprietary dataset from a major bank: - data/german_credit.csv - Public German Credit Data - data/features.csv - Engineered features

docs.title: Notebooks
docs.text:
• notebooks/01_data_exploration.ipynb - Exploratory data analysis
  • notebooks/02_feature_engineering.ipynb - Feature creation and selection
  • notebooks/03_model_training.ipynb - Neural network training
  • notebooks/04_explainability.ipynb - SHAP analysis and interpretation

  journal={SSRN Electronic Journal},
  year={2023},
  doi={10.2139/ssrn.3456789}
}

docs.title: Replication
docs.text:
To replicate our results:

  1. Install dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt
  2. Run notebooks in order: jupyter notebook
  3. Train model: python train.py
  4. Generate predictions: python predict.py

docs.title: Market Microstructure Analysis
docs.text: Code repository for "Price Discovery in High-Frequency Markets" (Journal of Finance, 2024).

docs.title: Authors
docs.text:
• David Lee (Quantitative Finance Department)
  • Sarah Williams (Trading Systems Lab)

docs.title: Abstract
docs.text: We analyze price discovery mechanisms in high-frequency trading environments using millisecond-level order book data from major exchanges.

docs.title: Published Papers
docs.text:
• Main Paper: DOI: 10.1111/jofi.2024.12345
  • Working Paper: arXiv:2201.67890

docs.text:
• data/orderbook/ - Limit order book snapshots (10ms frequency)

  • Total size: ~50GB (available via Zenodo: 10.5281/zenodo.1234567)

docs.title: GitHub Organization Research Platform
docs.text:
A comprehensive, automated research platform for academic GitHub organizations. Combines repository monitoring with advanced research features including publication tracking, citation analysis, reproducibility scoring, and community verification.

Live Demo: https://digital-ai-finance.github.io/org-dashboard/

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text:
• Automatic daily updates via GitHub Actions
  • Beautiful, searchable documentation site
  • Repository catalog with detailed information
  • Statistics and analytics
  • Organization by language and topics
  • Mobile-responsive design
  • Zero server costs (runs entirely on GitHub)

docs.title: Publication Tracking
docs.text:
• Automatic extraction of DOIs, arXiv IDs, SSRN papers from READMEs
  • Academic database integration (CrossRef, arXiv)
  • Publication metadata enrichment
  • Citation count tracking

docs.title: Code & Data
docs.text:
• Jupyter notebook rendering
  • Dataset detection and cataloging
  • Dependency analysis
  • Code language statistics

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text:
• Automated reproducibility scoring (100-point scale)
  • Badge system (Gold/Silver/Bronze)
  • Environment configuration detection
  • Docker support tracking

docs.title: Community Features
docs.text:
• Replication attempt tracking
  • Community verification system
  • Peer review ratings
  • Success rate metrics

docs.title: Advanced Search
docs.text:
• Full-text search across all repositories
  • Faceted navigation
  • TF-IDF relevance scoring
  • Autocomplete suggestions

docs.title: Visualizations
docs.text:
• Interactive Plotly charts
  • Citation network graphs
  • Publication timelines
  • Language distribution
  • Collaboration networks

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text:
• Data Fetching: Python script using PyGithub to fetch org data + research metadata extraction
  • Academic APIs: CrossRef, arXiv integration for publication enrichment
  • Analysis: Citation tracking, reproducibility scoring, search indexing
  • Markdown Generation: Jinja2 templates for dynamic content
  • Visualizations: Interactive Plotly charts, network graphs
  • Static Site: MkDocs Material theme
  • Automation: GitHub Actions for scheduled updates
  • Hosting: GitHub Pages (free)

docs.title: Live Demo
docs.text:
Visit our live demo at: https://digital-ai-finance.github.io/org-dashboard/

The demo includes 4 example repositories showcasing different research features:

  1. org-dashboard - This repository (meta!)
  2. portfolio-optimization-ml - ML research with arXiv papers and DOIs
  3. credit-risk-prediction - Neural networks with SSRN publications and notebooks
  4. market-microstructure - HFT research with Zenodo datasets and Docker

Features demonstrated: - Publications with DOI/arXiv links and citation counts - Reproducibility scores and badges - Community replication attempts and reviews - Dataset cataloging - Interactive visualizations

docs.title: 1. Create GitHub Personal Access Token
docs.text:
1. Go to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens > Tokens (classic)
  2. Click "Generate new token (classic)"
  3. Give it a descriptive name (e.g., "Org Dashboard")
  4. Select scopes:
  5. repo (Full control of private repositories)
  6. read:org (Read org and team membership)
  7. Click "Generate token"
  8. Copy the token immediately (you will not see it again)

docs.title: 2. Fork or Create This Repository
docs.text:
1. Create a new repository in your GitHub organization
  2. Clone this repository or copy all files to your new repo
  3. Push to GitHub

docs.title: 3. Configure Repository Secrets
docs.text:
1. Go to your repository Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions
  2. Add the following secrets:
  3. GH_PAT: Your GitHub Personal Access Token
  4. ORG_NAME: Your organization name (e.g., "my-org")

docs.title: 4. Enable GitHub Pages
docs.text:
1. Go to repository Settings > Pages
  2. Source: Deploy from a branch
  3. Branch: gh-pages / root
  4. Save

docs.title: 5. Run Initial Workflow
docs.text:
1. Go to Actions tab
  2. Select "Update Dashboard" workflow
  3. Click "Run workflow"
  4. Wait for completion (2-5 minutes)

docs.title: 6. Access Your Dashboard
docs.text: Your dashboard will be available at: https://YOUR_ORG.github.io/REPO_NAME/

docs.title: Update Frequency
docs.text:
Edit .github/workflows/update-dashboard.yml to change schedule:

schedule:
  - cron: '0 2 * * *'  # Daily at 2 AM UTC
  # - cron: '0 */6 * * *'  # Every 6 hours
  # - cron: '0 0 * * 0'  # Weekly on Sunday

docs.title: Customize Appearance
docs.text: Edit mkdocs.yml to change: - Site name and description - Theme colors - Navigation structure - Enabled features

docs.title: Customize Templates
docs.text: Edit files in templates/ directory to change: - Page layouts - Content structure - Displayed information

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • Git

docs.title: Install Dependencies
docs.text: pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Fetch Data Locally
docs.text:
export GITHUB_TOKEN='your_token_here'
export GITHUB_ORG='your_org_name'
python scripts/fetch_org_data.py

Or with arguments:

export GITHUB_TOKEN='your_token_here'
python scripts/fetch_org_data.py your_org_name

docs.title: Generate Markdown
docs.text: python scripts/generate_markdown.py

docs.title: Preview Site Locally
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

Then open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser.

docs.title: Build Static Site
docs.text:
mkdocs build

Output will be in site/ directory.

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
.
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       └── update-dashboard.yml  # GitHub Actions workflow
├── scripts/
│   ├── fetch_org_data.py        # Fetch data from GitHub API
│   └── generate_markdown.py     # Generate markdown from data
├── templates/                   # Jinja2 templates
│   ├── index.md.j2
│   ├── stats.md.j2
│   ├── repo.md.j2
│   └── ...
├── docs/                        # Generated markdown (committed)
│   ├── index.md
│   ├── stats.md
│   ├── repos/
│   ├── by-language/
│   └── by-topic/
├── data/                        # Generated JSON data (committed)
│   ├── repos.json
│   └── stats.json
├── mkdocs.yml                   # MkDocs configuration
├── requirements.txt             # Python dependencies
└── README.md

docs.title: Workflow Fails with Authentication Error
docs.text:
• Verify GH_PAT secret is set correctly
  • Ensure token has required scopes (repo, read:org)
  • Token may have expired - regenerate and update secret

docs.title: No Changes Detected
docs.text:
• Check if organization has any repositories
  • Verify ORG_NAME secret is correct
  • Check workflow logs for errors

docs.title: Pages Not Deploying
docs.text:
• Ensure GitHub Pages is enabled in repository settings
  • Check that gh-pages branch exists
  • Verify workflow has contents: write permission

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
• GitHub API has rate limits (5000 requests/hour for authenticated requests)
  • For large organizations, consider caching or reducing update frequency
  • Workflow implements basic rate limit handling

docs.title: Local Development Issues
docs.text:
Module not found: 

pip install -r requirements.txt

Permission denied: 

chmod +x scripts/*.py

Data files missing: Run fetch script first before generating markdown.

docs.title: Customization Ideas
docs.text:
• Add CI/CD status badges
  • Include code quality metrics
  • Show dependency vulnerabilities
  • Add commit activity graphs
  • Display contributor statistics
  • Track issue response times
  • Monitor PR merge times

docs.title: GitHub Actions Free Tier Limits
docs.text:
• 2000 minutes/month for free accounts
  • This workflow uses ~2-5 minutes per run
  • Daily runs: ~150 minutes/month
  • Well within free tier limits

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Contributions welcome! Please feel free to submit issues or pull requests.

docs.text: MIT License - feel free to use this for your organization.

docs.text:
Machine learning approaches to portfolio optimization using deep reinforcement learning

View on GitHub

docs.title: Financial markets and unemployment

Authors: Tommaso Monacelli, Vincenzo Quadrini, Antonella Trigari

docs.title: The Success of AdaBoost and Its Application in Portfolio Management
docs.text:
We develop a novel approach to explain why AdaBoost is a successful classifier. By introducing a measure of the influence of the noise points (ION) in the training data for the binary classification problem, we prove that there is a strong connection between the ION and the test error. We further id...

docs.title: Portfolio Optimization with Deep Reinforcement Learning
docs.text: This repository contains code and data for the paper "Deep Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization" (arXiv:2103.12345).

docs.title: Authors
docs.text:
• John Smith (University of Finance)
  • Jane Doe (Tech Institute)

docs.title: Abstract
docs.text: We propose a novel deep reinforcement learning approach for portfolio optimization that outperforms traditional mean-variance optimization. Our method uses a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to learn optimal trading strategies from historical market data.

docs.title: Published Paper
docs.text:
• arXiv: arXiv:2103.12345
  • DOI: 10.1016/j.jfineco.2023.01.001
  • Published: Journal of Financial Economics, 2023

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use this code or data, please cite:

@article{smith2023deep,
  title={Deep Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Optimization},
  author={Smith, John and Doe, Jane},
  journal={Journal of Financial Economics},
  year={2023},
  doi={10.1016/j.jfineco.2023.01.001}
}

docs.title: Datasets
docs.text:
• data/stock_prices.csv - Historical stock prices (S&P 500, 2010-2023)
  • data/portfolio_returns.csv - Simulated portfolio returns

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
numpy>=1.21.0
pandas>=1.3.0
tensorflow>=2.8.0
gym>=0.21.0

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text:
All experiments can be reproduced using:

python train_dqn.py --config configs/default.yaml
python evaluate.py --model checkpoints/best_model.h5

docs.title: References
docs.text: This work builds on: - Mnih et al. (2015) - Human-level control through deep reinforcement learning - Jiang et al. (2017) - A deep reinforcement learning framework for the financial portfolio

docs.title: Portfolio Optimizer
docs.text: An advanced AI-driven portfolio optimization engine that combines Modern Portfolio Theory with cutting-edge machine learning techniques.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Mean-variance optimization
  • Black-Litterman model
  • Risk parity strategies
  • ML-enhanced return predictions
  • Multi-objective optimization
  • ESG-aware portfolio construction

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install portfolio-optimizer

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
from portfolio_optimizer import Optimizer

optimizer = Optimizer()
weights = optimizer.optimize(returns, risk_free_rate=0.02)

docs.title: Risk Analysis Toolkit
docs.text: A comprehensive Python toolkit for financial risk assessment combining AI techniques with traditional statistical methods.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Value at Risk (VaR) calculations
  • Monte Carlo simulations
  • Credit risk modeling
  • Market risk analysis
  • Stress testing frameworks
  • AI-powered anomaly detection

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from risk_toolkit import RiskAnalyzer

analyzer = RiskAnalyzer()
var = analyzer.calculate_var(portfolio, confidence=0.95)

docs.title: Sentiment Analysis for Finance
docs.text: Advanced NLP-based sentiment analysis specifically designed for financial news, earnings calls, and social media to predict market movements.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Financial-domain BERT models
  • Real-time news scraping
  • Twitter sentiment tracking
  • Earnings call analysis
  • Multi-source sentiment aggregation
  • Market impact prediction

docs.title: Supported Sources
docs.text:
• Reuters, Bloomberg, WSJ
  • Twitter/X financial discussions
  • Reddit (r/wallstreetbets, r/investing)
  • Company earnings transcripts
  • SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from sentiment_finance import SentimentAnalyzer

analyzer = SentimentAnalyzer()
sentiment = analyzer.analyze_stock('AAPL')
